Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-13 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: momentum-ranker Today's data reveals two exceptional short-term momentum leaders with strikingly different risk profiles. TNGX dominates with a composite score of 98.1 (rank #1), driven by extraordinary returns—827% over one year and 169.65% over six months—while maintaining pristine technical structure (all SMA gates passed) and exceptional consistency across timeframes (ratio score of 98.34). However, TNGX's extreme outperformance raises a critical distinction: its momentum appears heavily concentrated in longer timeframes (ratio_1m_1w of 18.48 suggests a sharp acceleration), whereas RCAT shows more balanced growth across periods with 227% annual returns and stronger near-term resilience (9.4% advantage vs. SPY over one week). The correlation data reveals important divergences—TNGX is nearly uncorrelated with broad indices (QQQ correlation of 0.019) and negatively correlated with defensive sectors like Consumer Staples (XLP at -0.254), while RCAT shows positive correlation with commodities (IAU at 0.348), suggesting these are fundamentally different momentum drivers. Both pass all momentum quality gates and have clean technical signals, but TNGX's 807% edge over SPY on one-year returns represents an extreme outlier compared to RCAT
